items.title: Structuring a Gen AI codebase
items.description: Split an app into focused modules — prompts.py, clients.py, retrieval.py, tools.py — so each concern is testable and reusable, wired together with clear imports.

items.title: Using vendor SDKs
items.description: Import provider libraries (openai, anthropic, google.genai) and pull in only the classes/functions you use, keeping the namespace clean and dependencies explicit.

items.title: Fast cold starts with lazy imports
items.description: Defer heavy imports like torch or transformers into the function that needs them so CLIs and serverless handlers start quickly when those paths aren't exercised.

items.title: Script vs library dual-use
items.description: The __main__ guard lets one file act as both an importable module and a runnable CLI entry point — common for quick eval and demo scripts.

items.title: Curated package APIs
items.description: An __init__.py can re-export a small, stable public API (e.g. from .clients import get_client) so consumers import from the package root instead of deep internal paths.
